name: Ravenswood
description_short: Federal credit union in Ravenswood, WV with $46.2M in assets, 5,404 members, and 2 branch locations offering checking, savings, and loan products.
description_long:
Ravenswood Federal Credit Union is a community-focused federal credit union headquartered in Ravenswood, West Virginia, established in 1959. With 67 years of operating history, the institution has built a membership base of 5,404 members and maintains 2 branch locations across the region. The credit union is federally chartered (Charter Number 13321) and operates as a non-profit financial cooperative serving the community and surrounding areas.

The institution offers traditional banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their financial summary indicates they hold approximately $18.3 million in total loans across real estate, unsecured, and other loan categories, with $41.6 million in total shares and deposits from members. They employ 16 full and part-time staff members to manage operations across both locations and provide member services.

Ravenswood Federal Credit Union distinguishes itself as a locally-rooted institution with deep community ties in West Virginia. Their modest asset size of $46.2 million reflects a true community credit union model rather than a sprawling regional operation, allowing for personalized service and local decision-making. The institution maintains healthy financials with $268,248 in net income and a capital ratio of approximately 10%, demonstrating stability and member protection.

As a federal credit union, members benefit from NCUSIF insurance protection and access to credit union networks beyond their immediate branches. However, their limited size and 2-location footprint means members may face geographic constraints for in-person services compared to larger regional or national institutions. The institution is not FHLB-member designated and does not carry a low-income community designation, serving general membership eligibility criteria. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Money market or alternative share products (all other shares and deposits)
Other loan products (auto, equipment, or specialized lending)
Credit builder loans (implied in financial summary)
pros: NCUSIF-insured deposits providing federal protection up to $250,000 per member account
Not-for-profit credit union structure means earnings returned to members rather than shareholders
Local decision-making and community focus with 67-year operating history since 1959
ATM/debit card program with credit union network access beyond 2 local branches
Diverse loan portfolio including real estate, unsecured, and other loan products totaling $18.3M
Stable financial position with positive net income of $268,248 and 10% capital ratio
Low-cost wire transfer services mentioned as available offering
cons: Only 2 branch locations limits in-person access for members outside immediate Ravenswood area
Smaller institution ($46.2M assets) may offer fewer product options than regional or national banks
No FHLB membership indicates limited access to certain wholesale funding and liquidity tools
Limited online presence with basic website does not clearly detail all rates, fees, or digital banking capabilities
Employees compensation totals $753,274 for 16 staff, suggesting minimal staffing for member support volume
best_for: Ravenswood, WV residents and local community members seeking relationship banking and local decision-making
Small business owners and self-employed individuals needing flexible lending from a community-oriented lender
Members prioritizing not-for-profit structure and desire to keep financial relationships local
Individuals seeking NCUSIF-protected savings with credit union membership benefits and network access
diagnosis: Ravenswood Federal Credit Union is best for West Virginia residents in or near Ravenswood who prioritize local relationship banking and not-for-profit membership benefits over national convenience and extensive product selection. The main caveat is geographic limitation—with only 2 branches, members outside the immediate area face inconvenience, and the institution's smaller scale means fewer digital innovation and product variety compared to larger regional competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
